profile.institution_name: University of the Potomac-Washington DC Campus
profile.institution_unique_name: University of the Potomac-Washington DC Campus | Washington | District of Columbia

summary.tuition_dependence_vs_sector_median_sent: This college got 100% of its revenue from net tuition in 2024, above the median of 99% for private for-profit colleges.
summary.international_students_sentence: In 2024, 20.6% of students were international. That includes 10% of undergraduates and 37.2% of graduate students.

latest_status.action_labels: Required to Show Cause because of insufficient evidence of compliance with Standard II (Ethics and Integrity)

actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the self-study report. To note the on-site evaluation visit by Commission representatives to the main campus at 1401 H Street NW, Washington, DC 20005 on April 7-10, 2024. To note the following branch campus was visited: 7799 Leesburg Pike, Suite 200, Falls Church, VA 22043. To require the institution to show cause, by November 1, 2024, to demonstrate why its accreditation should not be withdrawn because of insufficient evidence that the institution is in compliance with Standard II (Ethics and Integrity), Standard VI (Planning, Resources, and Institutional Improvement), Standard VII (Governance, Leadership, and Administration), and former Requirements of Affiliation 6, 10, 11, 12, and 14. To note that the institution remains accredited while on show cause. To note further that federal regulations limit the period during which an institution may be in non-compliance, which starts on June 27, 2024. To require a show cause report, due November 1, 2024, documenting evidence that the institution has achieved and can sustain ongoing compliance with Commission standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, policies and procedures, and applicable federal requirements. The show cause report must include evidence of (1) the institution honoring contracts and commitments, adhering to policies, and representing itself truthfully, in all activities, whether internal or external (Standard II); (2) compliance with all applicable government laws and regulations and Commission policies and procedures, including but not limited to adverse or material actions by all its accrediting and licensing agencies and/or renewals or extensions of approvals by the Higher Education Learning Commission (HELC) and the State Council for Higher Education in Virginia (SCHEV), and other material issues which must be disclosed in a timely and accurate fashion (Standard II, Related Entities Policy and Procedures, and Third-Party Providers Policy and Procedures); (3) institutional and unit goals that are clearly stated, assessed appropriately, linked to mission and goal achievement, reflect conclusions drawn from assessment results, and are used for planning and resource allocation (Standard VI); (4) submission of the fiscal year 2023 audit confirming financial viability and proper internal financial controls, with evidence of corrective measures taken to address any material findings cited in the audit or an accompanying management letter (Standard VI); (5) documentation of the structure, authority, and autonomy of the governing body, including any related entities (Standard VII); and (6) a governing body that makes freely available to the Commission accurate, fair, and complete information on all aspects of the institution and its operations, including but not limited to any third-party provider or related entity, and specifically those entities referenced in the team's findings, and ensuring the institution describes itself in comparable and consistent terms to all of its accrediting and regulatory agencies (Standard VII, Accreditation Review Cycle and Monitoring Policy and Procedures, Related Entities Policy and Procedures, Third-Party Providers Policy and Procedures, and Public Disclosures Policy and Procedures) (Fourteenth Edition and Evidence Expectations by Standard Guidelines). To require that the institution complete and submit for approval, by November 1, 2024, a comprehensive, implementable teach-out plan and signed teach-out agreements with appropriate teach-out partner institutions (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education or transfer to another institution, if the Commission were to withdraw accreditation. To note the Commission may reject the teach-out plan and require resubmission if all of the required information is not provided. To direct an on-site show cause visit following submission of the report. The purpose of the on-site show cause visit is to verify the information provided in the show cause report and the institution's ongoing and sustainable compliance with Commission standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, policies and procedures, and applicable federal regulatory requirements. To direct the on-site show cause visit to include the following branch campus: 7799 Leesburg Pike, Suite 200, Falls Church, VA 22043. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss Commission expectations. To note that the institution will be invited to present before the Commission when it meets to consider its show cause report. To note that Recommendation Responses in conjunction with the Annual Institutional Update (AIU) are no longer required. To remind the institution of its obligation to provide to the Commission timely and accurate financial audits. The date of the next evaluation will be determined upon reaffirmation of accreditation. To note that additional reporting on Standard V will be required at a date to be determined dependent on the show cause outcome.
actions.action_label_short: Required to Show Cause because of insufficient evidence of compliance with Standard II (Ethics and Integrity)
